gold.rationale: 该样本的核心新闻事件系虚构：可用素材与原始证据一致指向的事实恰恰是2026年3月8日美国大多数地区仍按惯例在凌晨2点进入夏令时，而非被白宫紧急叫停。与此同时，文中多张图片并非真实记录这一“取消夏令时”事件的新闻现场，而是由生成图、后期加字编辑图或与政策无关的原图拼装成所谓证据，导致文本事实、图像内容和图文绑定关系同时失真，整体核心命题无法成立，应判为 False。

atoms: A large blue digital billboard displays the text 'Daylight Saving Time Canceled' and 'No Clock Change Tonight'.
The billboard is mounted on the exterior of a tall building with many lit windows.
People are standing in the foreground, some wearing winter hats and holding up phones to take pictures of the screen.
A sign above the building entrance reads 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION'.
Street lamps are visible on the left and right sides of the street.
The sky is dark blue, indicating it is nighttime or dusk.
A wooden grandfather clock is mounted on a wooden wall next to a brass Shortt clock.
A small white sign next to the clock reads '2:00 stays 2:00 tonight.'
A C-SPAN news banner is overlaid at the bottom of the image.
The banner text reads 'US Pauses DST Shift' and 'DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME CHANGE ON HOLD PENDING CONGRESS'.
A 'LIVE' indicator is visible in the bottom right corner of the banner.
The Shortt clock has a label describing it as a major breakthrough in timekeeping.
Two men wearing safety glasses are inspecting complex scientific equipment in a laboratory.
A sign on the wall reads 'Time Coordination Lab' and 'Federal Time Sync'.
A digital clock on the wall displays '14:37:52 UTC'.
A computer monitor shows synchronized time data for UTC, GPS, and TAI.
The equipment consists of vertical metal tubes and various cables.
A close-up view of an airport departure board.
The board lists destinations like Cleveland, Columbia, and Dallas.
A row for flight 'B74' is highlighted in orange with the text 'Cancelled'.
Other flights listed are marked as 'Delayed' with specific times.
The board has a blue background with white and yellow text.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A nighttime shot of a large blue digital billboard on a building facade. The screen displays white text announcing the cancellation of Daylight Saving Time. People are gathered in front, taking photos. The building below has a sign for the U.S. Department of Education.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: Daylight Saving Time Canceled No Clock Change Tonight U.S. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows a public announcement of the DST cancellation, matching the post's description of a large screen in a city.

visual_indicators.scene_description: An indoor shot of a Shortt clock and a grandfather clock mounted on a wall. A small sign indicates the time stays the same. A C-SPAN news overlay at the bottom reports that the US has paused the DST shift pending Congress.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: Stop #18 2:00 stays 2:00 tonight. LIVE C-SPAN c-span.org US Pauses DST Shift DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME CHANGE ON HOLD PENDING CONGRESS SHORTT CLOCK
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image features a news broadcast graphic explicitly stating 'US Pauses DST Shift', which aligns with the post's narrative.

visual_indicators.scene_description: Two men in a laboratory setting are examining high-tech time synchronization equipment. A sign identifies the room as the 'Time Coordination Lab'. A digital clock and a monitor display synchronized UTC, GPS, and TAI times.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: Time Coordination Lab Federal Time Sync RACK 2 14:37:52 UTC UTC 14:37:52 GPS 14:37:52 TAI 14:38:29
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The image shows technical equipment used for time synchronization, supporting the post's mention of federal coordination and technical adjustments.

cross_modal: image_1 is presented as visual evidence of the 'DST CANCELED' announcement on a public screen in New York City.
image_2 is offered as a portrait of the 'Shorttt Clock' device used to demonstrate the pause in the time change.
image_3 is used to support the claim that federal time synchronization labs are involved in the coordination.
image_4 is presented as a visual representation of the '2:00 stays 2:00' concept on a wall-mounted clock.
image_5 is used to illustrate the impact of the time change pause on flight schedules, specifically showing a cancelled flight.
image_6 is presented as a duplicate or alternative view of the Shorttt Clock device to reinforce the visual evidence of the pause.

cross_modal_findings.post_claim_about_image: The post claims this image shows a 'terminal prompt interface' or a 'federal timing display device' illustrating the pause in the time change.

retrieved_evidence.text: 美国多地在近年围绕夏令时存废展开讨论时，公共信息系统被频繁用于向民众传递通知。有关报道提到，一些城市的公共大屏或联邦大楼外电子告示牌以英文提示“Daylight Saving Time Canceled”或“No Clock Change Tonight”，强调原本在凌晨统一“拨快一小时”的做法将不再执行。据相关人员介绍，这类街头提示主要面向上下班通勤人群和游客，用以缓解制度变动对日常生活和工作安排的影响。
美国联邦层面的时间标准主要由国家标准与技术研究院等机构维护，其计时实验室承担原子钟、时间信号和公共系统同步的技术工作。近日有报道描述，在夏令时切换前夜，技术人员在实验室内对网络时间协议服务器、广播校时信号及部分关键基础设施的时钟系统进行紧急校核，以应对可能的政策调整。据相关技术专家介绍，一旦全国停止统一“拨快一小时”，这些联邦计时实验室需要重新规划跨州和跨行业的时间同步方案。
NEW YORK — Daylight Saving Time began Sunday, March 8, 2026, as clocks jumped from 2 a.m. to 3 a.m. local time across most of the U.S. Hawaii and most of Arizona do not observe the shift, per NIST.

BOSTON — Daylight Saving Time started March 8, 2026, moving an hour of daylight to the evening as clocks advanced at 2 a.m. The change lasts until Nov. 1, according to NIST. Many phones update automatically; manual clocks still need resetting.
美国夏令时制度传统上在每年春季的某个凌晨进行调整，多年来被设定在当地时间2点跳至3点，以减少对夜间活动的干扰。有媒体在近期报道中，以木质老式钟表和电视新闻字幕条为背景，解释“2点不再跳到3点”的含义，即联邦层面若停止统一切换，那么这一惯例节点将在部分州失去意义。据联邦及地方官员介绍，相关讨论也与睡眠健康、公共安全和设备同步成本等问题有关。
据美国政府相关人士近日透露，白宫与美国国家计量和计时相关机构在深夜召开紧急协调会议，并在会后宣布，自今年起不再执行春季将时钟拨快一小时的做法。消息人士称，这一决定通过行政通知形式下发各联邦机构和地方政府，要求在未来的时间调整季节维持统一标准时间。官方文件的具体措辞尚未完全公开，相关部门强调后续仍会根据实施效果进行评估。
美国公共计时设施中仍存在依赖机械结构或人工调整的设备，例如安装在校园、公园或行政建筑外的钟摆式计时装置。有关报道指出，在讨论叫停夏令时的过程中，这类设备被列为重点关注对象，因为其校时往往需要维护人员实地操作，而多次季节性切换增加了维护成本。据地方公共工程部门介绍，一旦全国不再统一切换，这些终端的人工校准频率有望减少，从而降低出错风险并提高社区时间显示的一致性。
据美国联邦政府内部人士透露，原定于当地时间3月8日凌晨2点进行的年度夏令时切换节点，在启动前不久被联邦部门紧急叫停。知情人士称，相关决定是在当夜通过应急程序下达的，多家关键基础设施运营单位接到通知后，暂停了预设的自动跳时操作。官方尚未就此发布完整说明，仅表示有关机构正在评估长期时间制度调整方案，后续安排将另行通告。
随着今年春季时间调整规则临时改变，多州居民本周在社交平台上发布大量个人生活照片和通知截图，称“今年不用调表了”。据当地媒体观察，这些帖文多与家庭作息、通勤安排和电子设备时间设置有关，一些用户还分享了与学校和公司收到的时间提醒。社交平台上的讨论据称在3月8日前后达到高峰，不少网民表示正在观望新制度对工作与生活节奏的实际影响。
美国主要交通枢纽，如大型机场和跨州铁路车站，长期依赖精确的时刻表和统一时间标准，以协调不同州和时区之间的航班、列车运行。近期在关于取消夏令时切换的报道中，有关部门被提到曾对航班信息屏和车站时刻牌进行重新核对，以确保跨州和跨时区的出发、到达时间与最新时间政策相符。据交通运营机构介绍，这类调整一旦与联邦计时机构的决策不一致，可能会对旅客行程和运输调度产生连锁反应。

WASHINGTON — The spring time change arrived March 8, 2026, with most Americans losing an hour of sleep overnight as clocks moved ahead at 2 a.m. Health experts say the shift can disrupt sleep and circadian rhythms, AP reports.
据交通和能源行业消息人士称，在联邦层面临时调整时间制度的通知发出后，美国各主要机场、铁路运营机构、电网调度中心以及证券交易和学校系统连夜收到统一对时指令。相关单位被要求在原定夏令时切换时点保持当前时间设置不变，以避免航班排期、列车时刻、电力负荷控制和交易结算系统出现混乱。多家机构称，技术团队已提前完成系统检查，确保关键业务在新时间安排下平稳运行。
多家电信行业内部人士近日表示，在联邦部门叫停夏令时切换后，各大型移动通信运营商紧急向用户设备推送系统补丁，以阻止部分智能手机在预定时间自动跳快一小时。据介绍，早期预设的软件仍保留原有夏令时规则，如不干预可能导致终端时间与官方时间不一致。运营商称，补丁在3月8日凌晨前逐步下发，大多数用户终端最终保持与网络时间同步，相关技术细节尚待进一步披露。
美国各州在时间政策上具有一定自主权，部分州早已通过本州法律表达对夏令时的支持或反对态度，例如要求采用全年统一标准时间或全年统一夏令时。联邦层面的任何决定，如果涉及自2026年3月8日等具体日期起停止全国统一“拨快一小时”，都需要与州政府及地方机构协调实施细节。据法律与政策研究机构介绍，这种协调不仅关系到钟表调整，还牵涉电力调度、广播节目编排、信息系统更新和民众生活习惯的适应过程。
美国多家公共卫生研究机构本周在内部评估报告中据称对夏令时制度提出质疑，认为季节性拨快时间对部分人群的心血管健康、睡眠质量以及儿童上学作息节律可能产生过大影响。有参与研究的卫生专家向媒体表示，这些担忧在过去几年中逐步累积，并在近期提交给联邦决策层，成为推动取消春季调表制度的重要参考因素之一。相关评估数据的详细结果尚未向社会全面公布。
在美国调整春季时间制度的消息传播后，部分涉外服务机构近日提醒，中国留学生、跨境商务人士和从事中美远程合作的群体需重新核对两国之间的时差，避免因夏令时规则变化导致航班、会议和线上课程安排出现偏差。据业内人士介绍，旅行社、航空公司和跨境支付平台已开始更新相关提示信息。留学中介建议学生关注学校官网和课程系统发布的新时间表，以便及时调整个人计划。
美国教育界长期关注时间制度对学生作息与学习表现的影响，特别是中学和大学阶段的早课安排。研究指出，春季夏令时切换后，青少年早起困难和课堂注意力下降的现象更为突出，引发家长和教师对“拨快一小时”制度的批评。近期有学区管理者表示，一旦全国不再统一进行夏令时切换，将考虑重新调整上课时间和校车运营表，以配合新的全年时间安排，并减少对学生健康和安全出行的干扰。
美国白宫在处理与时间制度相关的议题时，通常会与国会、各州政府以及联邦计时机构保持沟通。近年来，围绕“永久标准时间”或“永久夏令时”的立法提案多次在华盛顿被讨论，并伴随健康、教育和就业领域的研究报告。据公共卫生和劳工部门介绍，一些评估强调频繁时间切换对睡眠模式、学生上课表现和轮班工人事故率的潜在影响，为白宫和相关机构发布所谓健康与公共系统预警提供了政策背景。
美国医疗和公共安全机构在讨论夏令时政策时，往往聚焦心血管事件、交通事故和工业安全等指标的变化。有流行病学研究指出，春季时间向前调整后短期内心肌梗死、交通事故的风险有所波动，成为要求停止或改革夏令时制度的重要依据。据相关卫生部门介绍，白宫与联邦计时机构若联合发布健康预警，通常会提醒医院、急救系统和应急指挥中心关注特定时段的风险变化，以便在制度调整前后做好资源配置。

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post claims the DST switch was cancelled, while the evidence confirms the switch occurred as scheduled on March 8, 2026.
text_analysis.t1_evidence: The post claims the switch was cancelled ('美国正式取消夏令时'), but evidence [1] explicitly states 'Daylight Saving Time began Sunday, March 8, 2026, as clocks jumped from 2 a.m. to 3 a.m. local time'.
t1_problematic_subclaims: 不再执行春季拨快一小时的做法
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is fundamentally false because it asserts the cancellation of a scheduled event that the evidence confirms actually happened.

images.scene_description: A large blue digital billboard on a building facade displays white text announcing the cancellation of Daylight Saving Time, with people in the foreground taking photos.
images.ocr_text: Daylight Saving Time Canceled No Clock Change Tonight | U.S. DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
images.explanation: The image accurately depicts the scene described in the post and evidence: a public screen announcing the DST cancellation.

images.scene_description: A museum display featuring a Shorttt Clock device next to a wooden grandfather clock, with a C-SPAN news banner overlay at the bottom.
images.ocr_text: Stop #18 | SHORTTT CLOCK | 2:00 stays 2:00 tonight. | LIVE C-SPAN c-span.org | US PAUSES DST SHIFT | DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME CHANGE ON HOLD PENDING CONGRESS
images.explanation: The image is a genuine news broadcast still showing the relevant equipment.

images.scene_description: Two men in a laboratory setting are inspecting complex scientific equipment, with digital clocks on a monitor showing UTC, GPS, and TAI times.
images.ocr_text: Time Coordination Lab | Federal Time Sync | RACK 2 | 14:37:52 UTC | UTC 14:37:52 | GPS 14:37:52 | TAI 14:38:29
images.explanation: The image provides visual evidence of the technical coordination mentioned in the text.

images.scene_description: A close-up of an airport departure board showing flight statuses, with one flight marked as 'Cancelled' in orange.
images.ocr_text: C9 | B72 Delayed 9:45 AM | B74 Cancelled | C25 Delayed 2:00 PM | C27 Delayed 11:45 AM | C25 Delayed 2:00 PM | D1
images.explanation: The image supports the post's claim about the impact of the time change pause on flight schedules.

images.scene_description: A duplicate view of the Shorttt Clock device displayed in a museum setting.
images.ocr_text: Stop #18 | SHORTTT CLOCK | 2:00 stays 2:00 tonight. | LIVE C-SPAN c-span.org | US PAUSES DST SHIFT | DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME CHANGE ON HOLD PENDING CONGRESS
images.explanation: The image is a genuine photo of the equipment mentioned in the text.

judge.level3_rationale: 该帖文的核心主张——美国正式取消了夏令时切换——与事实完全相反，因为证据明确显示2026年3月8日夏令时切换如期进行，时钟确实从2点拨到了3点，因此该帖文属于完全虚假的信息。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post claims a cancellation that is explicitly refuted by evidence stating the switch happened as scheduled. The images support the false claim, creating a T1 fabrication and cross-modal inconsistencies.
judge.core_claim: The US has officially cancelled the Daylight Saving Time switch for the spring 2026 transition.
key_subclaims: The DST switch was cancelled at 2:00 AM on March 8, 2026.
The White House coordinated with federal agencies to pause the clock change.
Public screens and systems are displaying 'No Clock Change Tonight'.
The Shortt Clock and Time Coordination Lab images demonstrate the technical pause.
Flight B74 was cancelled due to the time change pause.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The DST switch was cancelled at 2:00 AM on March 8, 2026.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence [1] and [3] explicitly state that Daylight Saving Time began on March 8, 2026, as clocks jumped from 2 a.m. to 3 a.m. local time.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The White House coordinated with federal agencies to pause the clock change.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence [1] confirms the switch occurred as scheduled, contradicting the claim that it was cancelled.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Public screens are displaying 'No Clock Change Tonight'.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence [1] and [3] confirm the switch happened, meaning screens would show the change, not a cancellation.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The Shortt Clock and Time Coordination Lab images demonstrate the technical pause.
subclaim_findings.note: The images show a news broadcast about a 'pause pending Congress' (likely a past event or hypothetical scenario), but the text claims a finalized cancellation, creating a context mismatch with the current reality described in evidence.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Flight B74 was cancelled due to the time change pause.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence [1] confirms the time change happened, so the reason for the flight cancellation (time change pause) is false.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post's core claim that the DST switch was cancelled is directly contradicted by multiple evidence sources stating the switch occurred as scheduled on March 8, 2026. The images, while visually consistent with the post's narrative, are presented in a context that is factually false relative to the evidence. This constitutes a T1 Fabrication of the core event, combined with C2 and C3 inconsistencies between the post's narrative and the evidence.

image_findings.explanation: The image shows a screen announcing 'Daylight Saving Time Canceled', which contradicts the evidence stating the switch occurred as scheduled.
The image features a C-SPAN banner stating 'US Pauses DST Shift', which is a specific claim that contradicts the evidence confirming the switch happened.
The image shows a lab with synchronized times, but the context implies a 'pause' or 'cancellation' which is factually incorrect per evidence.
The image shows a cancelled flight, but the reason provided in the post (time change pause) is false because the time change occurred.
Duplicate of image 2, showing the same misleading context.
Duplicate of image 4, showing the same misleading context.
